expectations.text: Flags the shared VLAN as non-compliance with Control 1.1 (SWIFT Environment Protection — dedicated secure zone segregated from general IT)
expectations.evidence: "1.1 SWIFT Environment Protection — Mandatory ... Rating: Non-Compliant (NC) ... A shared VLAN with other internal systems does not constitute segregation."

expectations.text: Flags software OTP as a gap against Control 4.2 (MFA) and flags the logging state against Control 6.4 (no log review; 6-month retention is insufficient — SWIFT guidance expects at least 12 months)
expectations.evidence: "4.2 Multi-Factor Authentication ... Rating: Partially Compliant (PC) ... it is weaker than hardware-token MFA..." and "6.4 Logging and Monitoring ... Rating: Non-Compliant (NC) ... logs are collected but not reviewed... 6-month retention falls short of the ~12-month (or greater) expectation..."

expectations.text: Flags the absence of a SWIFT-specific incident response plan as a gap against Control 7.1 (Cyber Incident Response Planning)
expectations.evidence: "7.1 Cyber Incident Response Planning ... Rating: Non-Compliant (NC) ... A general IT incident response plan is a good foundation but does not satisfy this control, which specifically requires SWIFT-relevant scenarios and procedures..."

expectations.text: Correctly recognizes named individual operator accounts as compliant with Control 5.1 (Logical Access Control) rather than flagging them as a gap
expectations.evidence: "5.1 Logical Access Control — Mandatory ... Rating: Compliant (C) — provisionally ... Named individual accounts is exactly what this control requires and is a genuine strength."

expectations.text: Delivers a prioritized remediation plan (severity/priority ordering with concrete actions and rough sequencing), consistent with the requested gap-assessment format
expectations.evidence: "4. Prioritised Remediation Plan" with "Priority 1 — Critical (target: 0–30 days...)", "Priority 2 — High (target: 30–90 days)", "Priority 3 — Medium (target: 90–180 days)", each with lettered sub-actions (P1-A, P1-B, P2-A, P2-B, P3-A, P3-B), owners implied, and rationale ("Why first").
